summary: Expose Tella source upload/add-clip/transcript-cut APIs and add reversible compound video-edit workflow commands for full agent-operated edits.
reason: The live Tella OpenAPI gained POST /v1/sources, POST /v1/videos/{id}/clips, POST /v1/videos/{id}/clips/{clipId}/cut-by-transcript, and a multi-range body for POST /cut after the original print. These endpoints are the public API path for full editor coverage: uploading source assets, adding clips/B-roll sources, cutting by multiple time ranges, and cutting by transcript word indices. The novel workflow layer composes these primitives into insert-file, add-broll, silence-map, cut-words, replace-word-ranges, storyboard/apply-storyboard, format, audit, and playlist edit-pass so agents can operate the editor at video or playlist scope instead of juggling endpoint calls. The follow-up clean/undo layer adds a reversible per-clip edit workflow for fillers, silence buffers, trim edges, and optional mistake cuts.
validated_outcome: go test ./..., go build ./..., go vet ./..., verify-skill, and dry-run smoke tests for insert-file, add-broll, replace-word-ranges, apply-storyboard, and format pass.
